accuracyPage.metaTitle: How accurate is KickOracle? Euro 2024 + Copa 2024 backtest
accuracyPage.metaDescription: Independent retrospective accuracy of KickOracle's prediction model on Euro 2024 and Copa America 2024 — including Brier scores, top-1 accuracy, calibration curve, and a comparison vs FiveThirtyEight and bookmaker consensus.
accuracyPage.heading: How accurate is KickOracle?
accuracyPage.intro: Predictions without numbers are vibes. Below is the model's retrospective performance on Euro 2024 and Copa America 2024 — measured the same way bookmaker desks measure their own books, and compared head-to-head against FiveThirtyEight and devigged Pinnacle closing odds.
accuracyPage.tournamentTableHeading: Brier score · Top-1 accuracy · Log loss
accuracyPage.calibrationIntro: If we say a team has a 60% chance of winning, they should win about 60% of the time. Closer to the diagonal = better calibrated.
accuracyPage.ctaBody: Our prediction weights are public. So is our backtest. Read the full methodology and the input weights breakdown.

aboutPage.metaTitle: About KickOracle — Independent World Cup 2026 Intelligence
aboutPage.metaDescription: Meet the team behind KickOracle: independent football analysts building AI predictions, host-city travel guides, and team dossiers for the 2026 World Cup.
aboutPage.heading: Narratives over noise — and a clear paper trail.
aboutPage.intro: KickOracle is an independent football intelligence project built for the 2026 World Cup. We publish AI predictions, daily briefings, host-city travel guides, and team dossiers — without ads, without forum clutter, and without subscription traps.
aboutPage.missionBody: Most World Cup coverage is either cheerleading or coupons. We wanted a third option: clear, narrative-first analysis that respects the reader's time and shows its work. So we started KickOracle — a small, transparent operation with an unambiguous bias toward telling you what we actually think.
aboutPage.founderBio: TODO(content): Replace with a 100-150 word founder bio. Cover: relevant football credentials, why this project, what makes the methodology defensible. Add a real headshot at /public/about/founder.jpg and update the image src below.
aboutPage.analystBio: TODO(content): Replace with a 80-120 word bio for the predictions lead. Cover their stats/modeling background and which 2024 backtests they ran (Euro 2024, Copa 2024).
aboutPage.valuesHeading: What we will and won't do
aboutPage.willList3: Refund any unread purchase, no questions asked
aboutPage.willList4: Cite sources for every dossier claim
aboutPage.wontList1: Sell your data to bookmakers or third parties
aboutPage.wontList2: Run ads inside predictions or briefings
aboutPage.wontList4: Pretend to be neutral — we'll tell you what we think
aboutPage.contactBody: Press, partnerships, corrections, or analyst-applications: <link>email@kickoracle.com</link>. We answer every email.

predictionsPage.heading: World Cup 2026 AI Predictions
predictionsPage.description: AI-computed win probabilities for all 48 teams, combining chemistry index, form analysis, FIFA ranking, and intelligence signals updated daily.
predictionsPage.ctaText: Get daily win probability updates — free.
predictionsPage.premiumDescription: Get match-level AI predictions for all 104 fixtures, injury impact scores, live formation analysis, and early-access predictions before each match.
predictionsPage.methodFifaDesc: Why 40%? It's the only input with 30 years of out-of-sample predictive value. We anchor to it, then beat it on the other 60%.
predictionsPage.methodChemistryDesc: Squad cohesion the ranking ignores: shared minutes, club partnerships, and tactical familiarity. The biggest single edge over bookmaker consensus.
predictionsPage.methodMoraleDesc: Recent results, momentum, and locker-room signals. Captures hot streaks the FIFA ranking smooths out — and cold ones the market takes too long to price.
predictionsPage.methodStabilityDesc: Coaching tenure and squad continuity. Tournament football punishes new managers and rebuilt rosters; this weighting catches the contenders quietly already in trouble.

oddsPage.heading: Win Probability Analysis
oddsPage.description: Compare consensus win probabilities across all 48 World Cup 2026 teams. AI-powered edge detection highlights where the market diverges from our model.
oddsPage.valueBetsDesc: Teams where the AI probability model diverges from the consensus market baseline. A positive edge means the model rates the team higher than the market does.
oddsPage.methodDecimalOddsDesc: Decimal prices express market prices as probability signals. For example, a price of 6.00 implies roughly a 16.7% chance before provider margin.
oddsPage.methodImpliedProbDesc: The market's estimate of a team's chance of winning, derived from price signals. Calculated as 1 / decimal price. Lower prices imply higher probability. Note that the sum of all implied probabilities can exceed 100% due to provider margin.
oddsPage.methodMarketMovementDesc: <shortening>▲ Shortening</shortening> means prices are decreasing and implied probability is rising. <drifting>▼ Drifting</drifting> means prices are increasing and implied probability is falling. ▔ Stable means no significant change.
oddsPage.methodValueBetsDesc: Our AI model independently estimates each team's tournament win probability using squad chemistry, form signals, and historical performance data. When the model's probability diverges significantly from the market's implied probability, we flag it as a model edge.
oddsPage.disclaimer: Disclaimer: Probability data is for informational purposes only. KickOracle does not facilitate transactions or financial decisions.

guarantee.title: 7-Day Money-Back Guarantee

faq.heading: Frequently asked
faq.a1: Match Passes give you 48 hours of access from the time of purchase. Team Passes, Tournament Pass, and Scout Pass all remain active through July 25, 2026 — the end of the World Cup final.
faq.a2: Yes. Any money you've already spent on individual passes is credited toward your upgrade. Buy a few match passes, then upgrade to Tournament Pass — you only pay the difference.
faq.a3: We accept all major credit cards, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and select local payment methods through Stripe.
faq.a4: We back every pass with a 7-day money-back guarantee — if you haven't opened a single prediction, email support within 7 days for a full refund, no questions asked. Once you start reading content, the digital purchase becomes final.

developersPage.heroDescription: Match data, AI predictions, team signals, and market probabilities — delivered through a single REST API built for sports media, analytics platforms, and sports intelligence teams.
developersPage.step1Desc: Sign up and generate a key from the API dashboard. Keys are provisioned instantly after checkout.
developersPage.step2Desc: All endpoints require a Bearer token. Responses are JSON with consistent envelope format.
developersPage.step3Desc: Monitor usage, rotate keys, and manage billing from the dashboard. Rate limits scale with your tier.
developersPage.endpointsDesc: 15 endpoints covering the complete World Cup 2026 data surface. All responses follow a consistent JSON envelope.
developersPage.pricingDesc: Transparent pricing with no hidden fees. All plans include full API access at their tier level with instant key provisioning.
developersPage.customPlanText: Need a custom plan or white-label solution?
developersPage.featureAiPredictionsDesc: Composite models blending Elo, form, and social signals for match outcome probabilities updated in real time.
developersPage.featureRealTimeSignalsDesc: Injury reports, lineup changes, news sentiment, and social buzz aggregated from 50+ sources per team.
developersPage.featureConsensusProbabilityDesc: Pre-match and in-play probability signals from market consensus sources with movement history and edge detection.
developersPage.featureLowLatencyDesc: Edge-cached responses with sub-100ms P95 latency. Rate limits scale with your tier.
developersPage.featureWebhookEventsDesc: Subscribe to match kickoff, goal, lineup, and prediction update events via webhooks.
developersPage.featureOpenApiDesc: Full OpenAPI spec with interactive Redoc documentation. Generate typed clients in any language.
developersPage.startBuildingDesc: Free sandbox key available for evaluation. Production keys provisioned instantly after checkout.

verdict.elite: Tournament-defining talent — bookmark his every touch.
verdict.strong: Starting XI lock with a clear matchup edge.
verdict.viable: Squad asset — situational impact more than ninety-minute presence.

selectionProbability.title: Selection Probability
selectionProbability.subtitle: Opener Start & 26-Man Squad Odds
selectionProbability.defaultNote: Composite probability blends selection_risk, fitness state, and the latest playing-status overlay from our scouting desk.

verdict.lock: Locked into the opener XI — model treats this as a near-certain starter.
verdict.likely: Most likely a starter but not untouchable — watch the final session.
verdict.contested: Squad place uncertain — bench role or rotation is the base case.
verdict.bench: Outside the projected starting picture — squad slot itself is at risk.

communityArchive.title: Community Archive
communityArchive.description: KickOracle v1 is being narrowed back to narrative-first World Cup intelligence, so the old community/forum surface has been downgraded out of the main product path.
communityArchive.reason1: Sprint2 defines v1 as an intelligence product, not a social platform.
communityArchive.reason2: Comments, fan forums, and discussion-first loops were pulling attention away from the core team, match, and player narratives.
communityArchive.reason3: The archive remains reachable for reference while the main journey stays focused on reading and analysis.
communityArchive.note: If you are looking for the current product surface, start with the daily briefing, match board, or team dossiers.

communityCategory.title: {title} Archive
communityCategory.description: {title} is no longer an active discussion surface. KickOracle v1 is concentrating its primary experience around narrative-first World Cup intelligence instead of forum-first engagement.
communityCategory.reason1: {title} used to be part of the legacy community/forum graph, which is now outside the Sprint2 product boundary.
communityCategory.reason2: The active v1 journey prioritizes reading team, match, and player context instead of comment threads or discussion mechanics.
communityCategory.reason3: This category remains addressable only as an archived reference route while the main surface stays focused and buildable.
communityCategory.note: For current product flows, use the match board, daily briefing, and team pages.

volunteerPage.description: FIFA is recruiting over 30,000 volunteers across all 16 host cities for the 2026 World Cup. Volunteers play a vital role in delivering the tournament experience — from welcoming fans at airports to assisting with stadium operations.
volunteerPage.deadline: Applications expected to open late 2025 — register on FIFA+ for notifications.
volunteerPage.requirement2: Fluency in English required; Spanish and French are strong advantages
volunteerPage.requirement3: Available for at least 10 days during the tournament (June 11 – July 19, 2026)
volunteerPage.requirement4: Background check required for all positions
volunteerPage.requirement5: Legally authorized to volunteer in the host country (USA, Canada, or Mexico)
volunteerPage.requirement6: Previous event or sports volunteer experience preferred but not required
volunteerPage.benefit1: Official FIFA volunteer uniform and accreditation
volunteerPage.benefit3: Free public transit pass for match days in your city
volunteerPage.benefit6: Once-in-a-lifetime experience at the largest World Cup in history

roles.spectatorDescription: Welcome fans, assist with wayfinding, answer questions at stadiums and fan zones.
roles.transportDescription: Help manage fan flows at transit hubs, airports, and parking areas near venues.
roles.mediaDescription: Assist media representatives, manage press areas, support broadcast operations.
roles.protocolDescription: Support VIP and hospitality areas, assist with ceremonies and official events.
roles.techDescription: Assist with Wi-Fi, digital signage, VAR systems, and fan app troubleshooting.
roles.languageDescription: Provide interpretation and translation for international fans and delegations.

faq.a1: FIFA has not announced the exact date, but based on previous tournaments (Qatar 2022 opened 18 months before), applications are expected to open in late 2025. Register on FIFA+ to be notified.
faq.q2: Can I volunteer if I'm not a US/Canada/Mexico citizen?
faq.a2: You must be legally authorized to volunteer in the host country. Check with your embassy for specifics on visa requirements for volunteer activity.
faq.a3: Volunteers do not receive free match tickets, but you will be inside the stadium during matches in your assigned role.
faq.q4: Can I choose which city to volunteer in?
faq.a4: You can indicate your preferred city during the application, but assignments are based on need. Living near your chosen city improves your chances.
faq.a5: FIFA does not provide accommodation for volunteers. You are responsible for your own housing. Some cities may organize volunteer housing networks closer to the event.
faq.a6: All volunteers receive online training modules and in-person orientation sessions 2-4 weeks before the tournament begins. Role-specific training is provided for specialized positions.

geo.homeTldr: KickOracle is an independent, AI-powered World Cup 2026 prediction site covering all 48 qualified nations across the United States, Canada, and Mexico. We publish daily-updated win probabilities, squad chemistry analyses, host-city travel guides, and player intelligence reports — with a public methodology, public prediction weights, and a backtest disclosed against FiveThirtyEight and market-implied benchmark probabilities.
geo.homeFaqsAnswer1: KickOracle is an independent intelligence platform for the FIFA World Cup 2026. It publishes AI-powered win probabilities for every fixture, squad chemistry analyses, host-city travel guides, and daily briefings — covering all 48 qualified nations across the US, Canada, and Mexico from June 11 to July 19, 2026.
geo.homeFaqsHeading2: How are KickOracle's ratings and predictions calculated?
geo.homeFaqsAnswer2: Each team's win probability is a weighted blend of four public inputs: FIFA ranking (40%), squad chemistry (30%), morale (15%), and tactical stability (10%), plus tournament familiarity (5%). Player overall (OVR) ratings are derived deterministically from rating, age, fitness, position, caps, goals, and assists. The full formula and weights are published on our predictions and accuracy pages.
geo.homeFaqsAnswer3: Our model was retrospectively backtested on Euro 2024 and Copa America 2024 against FiveThirtyEight and market-implied benchmark probabilities, measured by Brier score, top-1 accuracy, log loss, and a calibration curve. The full backtest, including limitations, is published at /accuracy and was last updated on 2026-04-15.
geo.homeFaqsAnswer4: All daily briefings, team dossiers, host-city guides, the full match schedule, group analyses, and the Top-5 contenders predictions are free. A Tournament Pass unlocks the complete 48-team prediction table and premium dossiers. There is no advertising inside predictions or briefings.
geo.homeFaqsHeading5: How often are predictions and ratings updated?
geo.homeFaqsAnswer5: Predictions, power rankings, and player ratings refresh on a 5-minute server cache, with a daily editorial review for injury news, lineup changes, and form shifts. Live match pages refresh more frequently during kickoff windows.
geo.homeFaqsAnswer6: KickOracle's primary focus is the FIFA World Cup 2026 across the United States, Canada, and Mexico. After the final on July 19, 2026, year-round coverage extends to Champions League, the Premier League, Copa Libertadores, and a roadmap for AFCON 2027, the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027, and UEFA Euro 2028.
geo.homeFaqsHeading7: Is KickOracle affiliated with FIFA or any commercial prediction partner?
geo.homeFaqsAnswer7: No. KickOracle is an independent fan-built project. It is not affiliated with or endorsed by FIFA, and it has no commercial ties to third-party forecasting firms, leagues, or teams. All trademarks belong to their respective owners and are used for identification only.
geo.homeFaqsHeading8: Where does KickOracle's data come from?
geo.homeFaqsAnswer8: Match-level event data, FIFA rankings, public squad lists, and historical tournament results — combined with KickOracle's own derived metrics for chemistry, morale, and stability. Methodology weights are public; raw player metrics are computed in src/lib/player-derived-stats.ts.
geo.aboutFaqsAnswer1: KickOracle is built by a small, independent editorial and methodology team. The founder leads editorial direction, and a lead analyst owns predictions methodology and the backtests run on Euro 2024 and Copa America 2024. Contact the team at hello@kickoracle.com.
geo.aboutFaqsAnswer2: Yes. KickOracle has no commercial ties to FIFA, leagues, teams, or third-party forecasting firms. It does not run advertising inside predictions or briefings, and it does not sell user data to commercial data partners or third parties.
geo.aboutFaqsHeading3: What data sources do you use?
geo.aboutFaqsAnswer3: Public match-level event data, FIFA rankings, publicly available squad lists, historical international results, and KickOracle's own derived metrics for squad chemistry, morale, and tactical stability. We do not use proprietary scout reports or paid expert opinions.
geo.aboutFaqsAnswer4: Through a one-time Tournament Pass for premium predictions and dossiers, plus optional newsletter and affiliate links for tickets, travel, and merchandise. As an Amazon Associate, KickOracle earns from qualifying purchases. There is no venture funding and no league or prediction-market sponsorship.
geo.aboutFaqsAnswer5: Our prediction weights, backtest results, and rating formulas are published publicly, and our backtest compares head-to-head against FiveThirtyEight and market-implied benchmark probabilities. We disclose limitations openly on the accuracy page.
geo.accuracyFaqsAnswer1: We use three industry-standard metrics: Brier score (lower is better — measures the calibration and sharpness of probability forecasts), top-1 accuracy (the share of matches where the team given the highest probability actually won), and log loss (penalizes confident wrong predictions more than uncertain ones). All three are computed the same way professional forecasters evaluate probability models.
geo.accuracyFaqsHeading2: What does the calibration curve mean?
geo.accuracyFaqsAnswer2: If KickOracle says a team has a 60% chance of winning, that team should win about 60% of the time. The calibration curve plots predicted probability against observed frequency — points close to the diagonal mean predictions are well-calibrated; points above or below mean the model is over- or under-confident at that probability level.
geo.accuracyFaqsHeading3: How does KickOracle compare to FiveThirtyEight and market benchmarks?
geo.accuracyFaqsAnswer3: On the Euro 2024 + Copa America 2024 backtest, KickOracle is benchmarked head-to-head against FiveThirtyEight's published probabilities and market-implied benchmark probabilities. The full table on /accuracy shows Brier, top-1 accuracy, and log loss for each source on identical match samples.
geo.accuracyFaqsHeading4: Why backtest on Euro 2024 and Copa America 2024?
geo.accuracyFaqsAnswer4: These were the most recent senior international tournaments before the 2026 World Cup with full match samples and well-documented benchmark probabilities from FiveThirtyEight and public market baselines, making a like-for-like comparison possible. Backtesting against historical World Cups would risk look-ahead bias because the same player and team data is reused.
geo.accuracyFaqsHeading5: What does this backtest NOT show?
geo.accuracyFaqsAnswer5: It does not measure live in-match probability accuracy, does not control for the 2026 expanded 48-team format which has no historical analog, and does not predict individual player performances. The full list of limitations is published in the limitations section of the accuracy page.
geo.accuracyFaqsHeading6: Will the model be updated during the World Cup?
geo.accuracyFaqsAnswer6: Yes. The model parameters and weights are frozen before kickoff, but inputs (FIFA ranking, squad chemistry, morale, stability, fitness) refresh continuously throughout the tournament. We publish a post-tournament accuracy update once the final whistle blows on July 19, 2026.
geo.methodologyDataSourcesBody: Public match-level event data, FIFA rankings, publicly available squad lists, historical international results, and KickOracle's own derived metrics for squad chemistry, morale, tactical stability, and player fitness. We do not use proprietary scout reports or paid expert opinions.
geo.methodologyFormulaHeading: Rating formula at a high level
geo.methodologyFormulaBody: Each team's win probability is a weighted blend: FIFA ranking (40%), squad chemistry (30%), morale (15%), tactical stability (10%), and tournament familiarity (5%). Player overall (OVR) ratings are derived deterministically from base rating, age band, fitness status, position, caps, goals, and assists. Component ratings (PAC, SHO, PAS, PHY, DEF) are computed per-position.
geo.methodologyExclusionsBody: We do not use real-time market-price movements as a model input, do not weight social media sentiment for predictions, do not incorporate referee assignments, and do not adjust for in-match luck factors such as deflected goals or VAR overturns. Predictions are pre-match only.
geo.methodologyCadenceBody: Server-side caches refresh every 5 minutes, with a daily editorial review for injury news, lineup changes, and form shifts. The model formula and weights are frozen before tournament kickoff on June 11, 2026.
geo.methodologyGlossaryPacDef: Pace — derived from position-typical baseline, player age band, and fitness status; range 40-99.
geo.methodologyGlossaryShoDef: Shooting — driven by goals per cap (capped at 120 caps) and base rating; range 40-99.
geo.methodologyGlossaryPasDef: Passing — driven by assists per cap and base rating; range 40-99.
geo.methodologyGlossaryPhyDef: Physical — combines age band, fitness status, and base rating; range 40-99.
geo.methodologyGlossaryDefDef: Defense — anchored by position (GK 85, DEF 82, MID 65, FWD 42 baseline) and adjusted by base rating; range 40-99.
geo.methodologyGlossaryOvrDef: Overall — base rating × 10 with a small sentiment adjustment; range 40-99. The OVR is what most players, scouts, and fans recognize as the headline player rating.

teamStats.familiarityDesc: How long the squad has played together. Higher = more instinctive understanding on the pitch.
teamStats.stabilityDesc: How settled the coaching setup and formation are. Frequent changes lower this score.
teamStats.moraleDesc: Current squad confidence. Driven by recent results, fan support, and media sentiment.
teamStats.archetypeDesc: A past World Cup team whose playing style and trajectory most closely resemble this squad.

tacticalDNA.title: Tactical DNA
tacticalDNA.radarDescription: The radar chart shows six key dimensions of team strength. Bigger area = stronger overall squad.
tacticalDNA.atkDesc: Attacking quality: how dangerous the forwards are
tacticalDNA.midDesc: Midfield control: ability to dominate possession
tacticalDNA.defDesc: Defensive solidity: how hard they are to score against
tacticalDNA.chmDesc: Chemistry: how well the team plays as a unit
tacticalDNA.expDesc: Experience: average international caps per player
tacticalDNA.fitDesc: Fitness: percentage of squad fully fit for the tournament

teamDetailPage.metaDescription: AI analysis of {team}'s World Cup 2026 campaign. Group {group}, FIFA #{rank}. Squad, schedule, chemistry index, and win probability.

dimensions.attackHint: Forward line firepower — avg striker rating.
dimensions.spineHint: Keeper plus the two best defenders and midfielders.
dimensions.benchHint: Depth chart slots 12-23, the tournament's hidden fuel.
dimensions.formHint: Recent morale weighted with tactical stability.
dimensions.edgeHint: Where our model disagrees with the market consensus.

verdict.elite: Title-tier dossier — every sub-dimension cleared the cut.
verdict.strong: Quarterfinal-worthy with one or two cracks to manage.
verdict.viable: Knockout viable, but the edge is thinner than the bracket suggests.
verdict.limited: Round-of-16 ceiling — needs early variance to advance further.
